entry.title: New Learning Journey: Managing Products in the Backoffice Product Cockpit
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm Charis from SAP CX Product Learning team and I am excited to announce that we have published a new Learning Journey on learning.sap.com platform:

Managing Products in the Backoffice Product Cockpit 

The course focuses on beginner business users of SAP Commerce Cloud and the Product Manager role. It aims to teach how Product Managers use the Backoffice Product Cockpit to complete their daily activities.

Learning Objectives:

After the completion of the learning journey, the learners will be able to:

  • Understand the product data enrichment process.
  • Manage, categorize and classify products.
  • Search for products in Backoffice Product Cockpit and search using filters and/or facets.
  • Bulk create and edit products by using Excel import/export in the Backoffice Product Cockpit.
  • Understand Media Management Framework in SAP Commerce Cloud and the media conversion process.
  • Add and manage media assets, and assign media to products. 
  • Configure basic and multi-dimensional variants.
  • Group products into bundles and assign a special bundle price.

The learning journey contains very informative content provided in text, diagrams, videos. At the end of each unit, you will be called to answer on quiz questions which will test your understanding on the topic.

Accomplishing this learning journey earns you a SAP Authorized Badge, which will be claimed into your account!

entry.title: Re: Database module deployment failure
entry.content:
In BAS, open the terminal within your project and ensure you are in the db folder or project root where the package.json is located. Then run:

npm install @sap/hana-client --save
npm install @sap/hdi-deploy --save

This will ensure both @sap/hana-client and @sap/hdi-deploy are available

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM OData Analytics API for Configuration and Security Analysis
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM Configuration and Security Analytics

SAP Cloud ALM OData Analytics API for Configuration and Security Analysis is documented in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and in the official SAP Help documentation.

This API provides access to analytics data of Configuration and Security Analysis use case of SAP Cloud ALM. It exposes the same data than the generic Analytics OData API (DataSet) and the REST Analytics API.

The API support several use cases:

  1. Access the configuration stores and elements 
  2. Retrieve the number and type of historical changes 
  3. Check configuration compliance against security recommendations
  4. Access the full content of a configuration store (items key and value) (*)

(*) The API provides access to the full contents of a configuration store for a single store only. 

In the following we'll show how this analytics OData API can be used with SAP Analytics Cloud by building step by step a simple story. 

We take as example a system of type "SAP NetWeaver AS for ABAP" with id EE1.

 

In order to get the data in SAP Analytics Cloud we need a simple query which selects only the serviceId:

https://{{CALM_URL}}/api/calm-analytics/v1/odata/v4/analytics/ConfigurationItems?$filter=period eq 'C24M' and serviceId eq 'c19e8958-92fa-422b-921b-f9349f2ddfda'

With filter "serviceId eq 'c19e8958-92fa-422b-921b-f9349f2ddfda'" we select the service EE1 of type "SAP NetWeaver AS for ABAP" using its LMS-ID (Landscape Management Service Id). This id can be retrieve from the LMS application of SAP Cloud ALM but also looking at the URL parameters in CSA application (see screenshot below).

With filter "period eq 'C24M'", we select a large period of time of 24 months (including the current month since the period start with character 'C' and not 'L' for LAST). The value of the period to use depends on how far you want to go to build historical charts. If you are not interested by historical values (which are identified with dimension "ElementStatus=OLD"), you can just ignore filter "period" and add the filter "ElementStatus=CURRENT".

Remark about API parameters used by SAP Analytics Cloud

If we call the URL indicated above using an API tool (and the proper OAuth 2.0 credentials / token), we receive all dimensions and measures exposed by the CSA analytics provider:

This includes the dimensions "itemKey" and "itemValue" which are null since we don't access a single configuration store but all the configuration stores of system EE1. When the OData API is called from SAP Analytics Cloud (see after), we generally use the graphical editor to construct the query with the graphical editor. SAP Analytics Cloud adds additional parameters like "$select" which lists the fields (dimensions and measures) that should be returned by the OData API.

SAP Analytics Cloud Connection

There is nothing specific about the SAP Analytics Cloud connection you need to access the CSA OData Analytics API. You can re-use an existing SAP Cloud ALM connection for the generic OData Analytics API or create a new one if you don't have one already.

Of course you need a proper OAuth 2.0 credentials (client id, client secret) with at least the minimum required security scopes:

  • calm-api.analytics.read
  • calm-api.csa.read 
  • calm-api.csa.personal.read

Data Service URL: https://calm-demo.eu10.alm.cloud.sap/api/calm-analytics/v1/odata/v4/analytics/

Token URL: https://calm-demo.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

SAP Analytics Cloud Model

The SAC model can be created in few clicks with minimum effort.

To create the OData model select the connection to the SAP Cloud ALM tenant we want to access.

We select the Entity "ConfigurationItems".

 

We select all dimensions and measures except "itemKey" and "itemValue" that won't be used since we want to create a model with all the configuration stores of the service EE1.

The three measures are automatically recognized as such.

  

 

 

Dimensions "elementDate" and "elementDateTo" are automatically recognized as Timestamp. In this example, we change the type of  those two dimensions to Date since it is more convenient to use in the story and we don't care about the time resolution.

The API returns two additional control dimensions:

  • period: input semantic period with format [L|C]<number[D|W|M|Y]
  • timeZone: input time zone (by default UTC is used)

SAP Analytics Cloud Story Example

Number of configuration stores

One option to count the number of configuration stores is to count the number of storeIds.

Number of configuration items 

To report on the number of items per configuration store we use dimension "StoreName" and measure "NumItems". To count only the current number of items and not the historical ones, we use filter "ElementStatus=CURRENT".

Compliance

In the example below we check the configuration compliance against the security recommendations. To do so, we select only the current version of the configuration by applying the filter "ElementStatus=CURRENT". We use the measure "NumItems" to count the number of items per compliance status and the dimension "ItemSecRecStatus" to distinguish betwen configuration items COMPLIANT, NON COMPLIANT and the those which are not rated.

 

 

Change history

In this example we display the number of changes per week. To do that we use dimension "ElementDate" together with measure "NumChanges". We can easily construct this historical view since we have converted the type of dimension "ElementDate" from Timestamp to Date (see model construction above).

entry.title: Re: Set SAPGui 760 LandscapeFileOnServer with SAP Event Scripts
entry.content: [deleted]

entry.title: Re: Set SAPGui 760 LandscapeFileOnServer with SAP Event Scripts
entry.content:
Include a powershell command during installation, or push it to all devices.

<<new-item -path "HKLM:\Software\wow6432node\SAP\SAPLogon\Options" 

set-itemproperty -path "HKLM:\Software\wow6432node\SAP\SAPLogon\Options" -name "LandscapeFileOnServer" -value "\\path\to\file.xml">>

This will populate for all users, regardless if they are existing or new. (This will not affect users with the value already set through the GUI or HKCU registry)

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = ("202501","202502","202503")

MEMBERSET [d/Y_FUNCAREA] = ("4100","5110","7130","4250","7280")

VARIABLEMEMBER #X OF [d/Y_FUNCAREA]

FOREACH.BOOKED [d/Y_FUNCAREA]

DATA([d/Y_FUNCAREA] = #X) = RESULTLOOKUP()

entry.title: Re: Update Header text of PR through Interface
entry.content: These texts are called long texts, and they can be stored in tables via SAVE_TEXT. You can find the documentation in the SAP Library and in all the existing answers.

entry.title: Re: Login Failed after upgrade from FP18 to FP20 PL03

This has now changed. Technical users can no longer log on to the SAP Customer Checkout manager user interface. Kind regards, Gunther

entry.title: Need help with printing of an https:// URL in background without reading the contents
entry.content:
Hi Experts, Please suggest how can we print a web page ("https://www.xyz.com/id=12345" available over the internet) while printing an Adobe form in a background process.

Detail: When production order is released, we are printing an Adobe form with data. Along with this, a URL page over the internet should also be printed (first 2 pages are from Adobe form, rest of the pages should be the printout of URL page). The owner of the URL page does not have a public API, which we can call and get data. The data is related to details of a material from an e-commerce website. 

We tried to read URL contents using class CL_HTTP_CLIENT. Response of the HTTP request returns source page with tables and images , & corresponding HTML tags and CSS scripts. Here, we extracted the data using ABAP string manipulation ( reading texts like <table>, <img..> , <tr>, <td> etc, ) and filled internal tables. Then, we printed contents using an Adobe form. The problem is that we can't hard code tag names in ABAP code because owner of the web page can change its technology in future without informing us and then code wont be able to read URL page contents.

We tried with class CL_GUI_HTML_VIEWER, but could not print URL page in background, because it involves module pool screen. As per discussion with middleware team, they can also extract data from a webpage, but would need to write tag names in code. 

Hence, please suggest if there is a way to print a URL page in background without reading its contents. 

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: BRIM-The subscriber account "00000*****" (with "BRIM"...
entry.content:
Hello Guys,

Issue got resolved.

S4HANA - 2023

  • SAP CC 2022 (is connected to S4HANA 2023).
  • Maintain Web service : CO_FKKCC_SUBSCRIBER_ACCOUNT (not for *V1 version) unless if you have any specific additional fields to be populated into CC.
  • Maintain "Customer Management Area" in CC -  CMA Name should be of "<S4HANASID>_<client>" (Ex: S4H_100) and select the ERP Destination (configured in CC)
  • Take the updater restart
  • Submit the Order with new created BP in SOM and Submit it
  • You See the BP, CA & Subscription Contract's are distributed into CC successfully

Note: If BP is not replicated with the same error. Switch ON the DEBUG for Updater in CC and try pushing the data from SOM. Find the .trc logs and see what is the error. Else create the OSS ticket to SAP.

Thanks,

Rahul

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager for Windows 3.0 Now Available – What’s New?
entry.content:
The SAP Mobile Service and Asset Management team is pleased to announce the 3.0 release of SAP Service and Asset Manager for Windows.

Our Mobile Development Kit (MDK) for Windows team continues to deliver the components and capabilities needed to process standard metadata and build a native Windows user interface. This is the framework that powers SAP Service and Asset Manager, and this is the first native Windows application developed using the cross-platform MDK.

Please read on to learn more about our latest release.

Compatibility

This update is based on the 2405 feature set and metadata from the principal release and supports maintenance technician, field service technician and safety technician personas.

Please see 3525566 - Software Release Note – SAP Service and Asset Manager 3.0 for Windows for detailed information. Additional information is available in the SAP Help Portal.

New features in the 3.0 release

Safety Technician persona

Introduced in the 2305 release of SAP Service and Asset Manager, the Safety Technician persona ensures the safety of workers when working with machinery or equipment that could pose a risk of injury or accidents during maintenance. Integrated with SAP Work Clearance Management* (WCM) on SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA, the persona focuses on the safety lockout-tagout (LOTO) process.

The persona has a custom user experience optimized to easily find operational items and certificates on the home page. The technician may access details about work approvals, permits, work orders, equipment, and functional locations. 

Learn more about the Safety Technician Persona in SAP Service and Asset Manager

Standard and Professional Personas

The SAP Service and Asset Manager maintenance technician persona enables detailed end-to-end maintenance execution procedures on a mobile device, however, some customers want a simpler mobile application where technicians and floor supervisors may report and manage maintenance requests with access to asset data and related details

This release supports standard and professional categories for our maintenance and field service personas. The streamlined standard personas are licensed at ½ FUE, and professional personas at 1 FUE.

Learn more about persona configuration on the SAP Help Portal

Maintenance technician

  • Professional: Same long-standing persona from previous releases; absolutely no change in previously available features or functionality.
  • Standard: Focused on notification processing with features like core data, measurements, and attachments; does not include advanced features such as supervisor role, signature capture, and expenses, geospatial, nor integrations like linear asset management and routes.

Read more about Maintenance Technician Standard Persona in SAP Service and Asset Manager 

Field service technician

  • Professional: Includes all features and functionality in the standard persona; adds meter management capabilities, technical object bill of materials and classifications, and the ability to create/edit technical objects.
  • Standard: Same persona features and functionality available since the 2.0 release.

Integrations and components

SAP Dynamic Forms‡ support

A recent addition to our SAP Mobile Service and Asset Management portfolio, SAP Dynamic Forms provides a platform for creating and managing digital forms built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Templates can be designed with form fields and validation rules and then assigned to work orders and operations. As part of the service, the forms may be accessed in a web browser or directly from SAP Service and Asset Manager.

Form instances associated with work orders, operations, and suboperations will be download to the device and may be completed offline during work execution. Technicians may save drafts as they complete their work, submitting the final form and syncing after completion.

Completed forms are synced back to SAP BTP where post-processing can occur, such as mapping captured data to business objects and triggering follow up actions.

Learn more about the 1.0 release of SAP Dynamic Forms

Smartforms† support

This integration allows the mobile client to download, modify, and upload Smartforms. These can be dynamic, multi-step forms used to capture safety data, inspections results, notes and much more. A solution from SAP Field Service Management, Smartforms are managed in the Smartforms and Feedback app. 

Controls and functionality

Quick filters and tabs for lists

Across the application, list pages now include the intuitive filter feedback bar, offering users a convenient way to manage filters. Positioned above the object list, this horizontal scroll area hosts interactive chips that display applied filters and available options.

Quick action buttons with personalization

Quick action buttons are strategically positioned at the top of detail pages. These context-aware buttons minimize unnecessary navigation and reduce the number of clicks required to perform essential tasks.

These buttons may be customized based on user preference. A settings icon appears next to each button set where buttons may be disabled if desired. Further, the buttons are customizable on the business level to accommodate diverse customer-specific use cases.

Deep linking

Deep linking enables you to navigate to a specific screen or functionality within the app via a URL or link, which can be very useful for user workflows. For instance, you can create a link that opens a particular work order or service order directly in the SAP Service and Asset Manager app.

Submit your ideas

We appreciate the early-adopter customers who have given us much feedback on the Windows client. I encourage everyone to learn more about How Customers and Partners Can Submit Improvement Requests. Windows requests can be tagged using the Windows category.

Release available now

The Windows 2.0 application and metadata are available for customers and partners from the SAP Download Center. Navigate to SAP SERVICE AND ASSET MANAGER > MOB SERVICE ASSET MANAGER WIN > MOB SVC ASSET MANAGER WIN 3.0 > MOB SVC ASSET MANAGER WIN 3.0.

The client application is also available in the Microsoft Store and includes a demo mode for exploring.

Explore other SAP mobile applications

SAP has a powerful suite of native mobile applications. Follow the MXE Community for latest updates.

* SAP Work Clearance Management is required for the Safety Technician persona and is licensed separately.

† SAP Field Service Management is licensed separately; Smartforms is not included with SAP Service and Asset Manager.

‡ SAP Dynamic Forms is included with SAP Service and Asset Manager license with fixed entitlement of 1200 documents per FUE/year; tiered add-on licensing available for blocks or 1000 documents.

entry.title: SuccessConnect 2024-Every employee is a success story with SAP BTP
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform at SuccessConnect 2024

Exciting news, demos and information on innovation, integration, extending HR apps, AI, analytics and workforce planning

It’s time to get ready for this year’s SAP SuccessFactors SuccessConnect event!  This year’s sold-out event takes place in Lisbon, Portugal October 28 through the 30th and features more SAP Business Technology Platform-related sessions than ever before – 18 sessions including one special session on workforce planning available for free in the virtual event!

Discover solutions designed to fuel employee and business success and strategies for improving your employee experiences, decisions, and processes.

To help guide attendees in packing their agendas with as much SAP BTP content and information as possible, here’s a guide of what is happening. Add these sessions to your SuccessConnect agenda now!

For more information on this year’s SuccessConnect event, visit https://www.sap.com/events/successconnect.html or to learn more about how SAP Business Technology Platform can help HR visit http://www.sap.com/btp-for-hr .

General and Business Technology Platform

  • Using SAP Integration Suite to connect HR with the business
    Learn how SAP Integration Suite can help you connect HR across the business by integrating SAP SuccessFactors HCM with other SAP and third-party applications. Hear how organizations of all sizes are using this approach to create a single source of people data, enable end-to-end processes, and drive automation and efficiency. SAP Integration Suite 

  • Using SAP Build to extend your SAP SuccessFactors solutions
    Standardized business processes have come a long way, but every company still has a few things it does differently from everybody else. SAP Build solutions can help extend your human capital management solutions to address your specific business needs. See firsthand how to create low-code/no-code apps that boost end-user adoption and efficiency. SAP Build 

  • Road map and vision: Technology Foundation
    Explore the latest innovations and enhancements in the technology foundation of SAP SuccessFactors solutions, including workforce analytics, security, integration, extensibility, and more. Gain insights into what we have planned for our upcoming releases and get your questions answered by our product experts.

  • See how new innovations help you integrate and extend your SAP SuccessFactors solutions
    Discover how our recent innovations around domain-driven APIs and push events can help transform your business operations and enhance the user experience. Learn how you can use these technologies to achieve near real-time integrations with external service providers and streamline your business processes

  • Build trust with security and identity management technologies
    Discover the key technologies and innovations to help you secure your people data. Explore key challenges and learn how to mitigate risk and develop trust with a deep dive into newly available capabilities, including customer-managed keys in the new advanced encryption add-on and enhancements to the role-based permissions tool.

  • Unlock innovation with the generative AI hub
    The new generative AI hub in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad makes it easier for developers to build applications securely and efficiently with access to a broad range of tools and large language models. See how tools for prompt engineering, experimentation, and other AI capabilities can accelerate your development of applications for HR and more.

 

Analytics and Workforce Planning

  • Hands-on-lab: Create compelling report stories in SAP SuccessFactors HCM
    Designed for reporting administrators, this session will help you learn how to use a single, unified reporting approach to tell compelling stories. Get hands-on experience with key capabilities, such as rich visualizations and intelligent data joins, and learn how to use live data from SAP SuccessFactors solutions. This session is intended for intermediate to advanced reporting administrators with a working knowledge of the report center and role-based security permissions. It is for existing customers only. Laptops and scripts will be supplied. Note that registration is required for this session. Space is limited so that speakers have time to answer attendee questions. If a registered session attendee is five minutes late to the session, we will give their seat to the next person waiting in line. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • Road map and vision: Reporting and analytics
    Explore the latest innovations in analytics and the significant benefits they offer for your business. Discover how to make use of these enhancements by finding out more about our new SAP SuccessFactors Analytics solution, report stories within SAP SuccessFactors solutions, and workforce planning in SAP Analytics Cloud for planning. SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • Improving people sustainability with workforce planning and data analytics
    With skill gaps increasing and regulatory mandates growing more complex, CHROs are turning to longer-term people sustainability. Discover how HR leaders can use AI-driven analytics and strategic workforce planning to create a workforce of the future. Gain insights on using skills analysis to inform talent investments and improve workforce planning. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • Get the mot out of workforce planning templates using SAP Analytics Cloud for planning
    SAP Analytics Cloud for planning includes workforce planning templates. Get an overview of the various templates available in the business content network and the planning processes they support. Whether you’re looking to do operational, strategic, or skill-based workforce planning, these insights can help you. SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • Top three ways to create dynamic operational report stories
    How do you best create stories as unfolding narratives for your users to further explore data in more detail? Gain practical tips on how to use report stories within SAP SuccessFactors solutions to create dynamic, interactive stories that let users dive into details as needed. SAP Analytics Cloud 

 

Customer Speaking Sessions

  • How Eurobank uses SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking and SAP BTP to comply with local labor legislation
    To maintain compliance with a new labor law and avoid costly fines, Eurobank chose the SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking solution and SAP Business Technology Platform. Learn how the financial organization accurately tracks employee work hours, shifts, and overtime while linking data in real time to third-party platforms.

  • Unleashing HR insights: Reporting and workforce analytics at Dr. Oetker
    See how reporting in SAP SuccessFactors solutions has transformed Dr. Oetker’s HR reporting landscape and explore real-world use cases in the SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Analytics solution. From overcoming challenges to unlocking transformative insights, discover the power of reporting and analytics capabilities of SAP solutions. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • How agile HR transformation at Volkswagen increased performance and transparency
    Using the “SAFe” framework, Volkswagen implemented SAP SuccessFactors solutions in one of the largest HR transformation programs worldwide. Join this session to learn how the company established a new product organization to increase performance and transparency.

Demo stations

  • DP106: HR analytics and workforce planning area
    Discover how you can improve company performance and decision-making with data-driven people insights, workforce planning tools, and AI-driven recommendations. SAP Analytics Cloud  SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • DP105: System administration and extensibility area
    See how SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) can help you grow your capabilities with third-party integrations and custom extensions featuring a real-world demo of a re-envisioning automated equipment ordering with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central driving real-time triggers to order new hire equipment in SAP Ariba via SAP Integration Suite with automated service ticket create in SAP Service Cloud. SAP Integration Suite  SAP Build

entry.title: Latest Features of Calibration in 2H/2024 Release cycle
entry.content:
Calibration in SAP SuccessFactors is a critical feature for organizations striving to maintain fairness and consistency in performance evaluations. It allows HR teams and managers to review and adjust employee ratings, ensuring that performance and potential are assessed equitably across the board. This process helps eliminate biases, aligns performance ratings with organizational goals, and facilitates more informed talent decisions.

With the 2H/2024 release, Calibration has been enhanced with exciting new features that bring more precision, transparency, and efficiency to the process. From AI-driven insights to improved user interfaces, this update empowers organizations to make smarter, data-backed decisions about their workforce. Whether you're a manager looking for more flexible calibration sessions or an HR leader focused on diversity and fairness in evaluations, these new features are set to transform the way you approach performance management.

In this article, we'll take a deep dive into the latest updates in Calibration, highlighting the key innovations and how they can benefit your organization.

1. Enhancements to AI-Assisted Writing and Availability in More Areas -  Improve the comments provided by session reviewers to subjects.

Two new options are available now which are Translate and Analyze Text. The 'Translate' option would help in converting the given text into any of the 146 languages(that particular language enablement from the provisioning end is required). Also, the 'Analyze Text' option would exclude inclusiveness and other harmful content from the fields. See more

2. Set Numerical Distribution in Calibration Templates - You can now set numerical guideline in addition to the percentage guideline in the calibration sessions. If both, numerical and percentage guidelines are applied, the numerical guideline would be given priority. If percentage guideline also does not exist, no guideline will be applied.

For a numerical guideline to be applied, the total number of subjects should match any of the guideline created. Also, you can only create one guideline for a specific number of subjects. For instance, if you have created a numerical guideline for 7 number of subjects, 'Excellent=2,Good=3,Poor=2', you cannot have another guideline for 7 subjects, 'Excellent=1,Good=3,Poor=3'.

Also, when you switch the template, two things have to be kept in mind, for keeping the numerical guidelines intact in the new template:

  1. The rating type for the two templates must be identical.
  2. The rating scales, including labels, for each rating type within each template must be identical.

This features further regulates the process and is useful if the group is smaller. See more

3. New Setting for Configuring Autosave - In the last release, a feature was developed to enable autosave during the calibration if no activity is detected for around 15 seconds. It was enabled by default. Now, with this release, you can turn off this feature from 'Manage Calibration Settings'. Earlier, you could not turn off this autosave. See more

4. Export Calibration Sessions to an XLSX File - You can now export calibration sessions in XLSX format, which accommodates up to 16,384 columns. In the past, exports were limited to the XLS format, which could only handle 256 columns. See more

5. Turn Off Email Notification for Rating Changes During Session Finalization - If you want to turn off the notification sent to managers whenever there is a rating change, you can do it from the 'Email Notification Template Settings'. This will turn the notification off for all the templates created. Earlier, it was required to do it from the template level separately for each template in Manage Calibration Templates -> Advanced Tab -> deselecting the option Manager notification with rating changes on session finalization. See more

Thanks for reading my article. Please provide your insights in the comments.

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services as of July 2024
entry.content:
Hi Najari,

Your points have been noted and we have taken them into consideration.

Best regards,

Shiba.

entry.title: Change output to a different Datastore in existing task?
entry.content: Currently have a task where the output is written to SAP_IBP. I am in need of writing the output to a flat file in CSV format. Is there an easy way to copy the task and define a new target Datastore?

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services as of July 2024
entry.content:
Hello Sang,

The Data Migration contents re built on SAP Data Services.They are not a part of the Data Services product. Anything built on Data Services and upgraded to the respective GA releases should work on the PCE version of the SAP Data Services.

Best regards,

Shiba

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA trial on GCP with 90-day, $300 Free Trial offer
entry.content:
Hi LaMi ,

During appliance creation in Basic Mode the recommended VM Sizes have been applied. In case that you create an appliance via Advanced Mode Wizard, you will be able to choose additional VM Sizes, which are applicable, but the best fitted are preselected as a default choice. Each appliance temple has own VM Sizes template depending on the deployed software and required resources. For more information, see our documentation .

The mention by you GCP related error (Quota 'SSD_TOTAL_GB' exceeded) means that you do not have enough quota in your GCP account. Whether the quota adjustment is in line with GCP offer is question related to the GCP Support.

 Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: Re: Reverting Container to the latest published version CLEARS ALL COMPONENTS of my app
entry.content: You suggested before that for a successful build all components should be reverted to the latest published version, but if I revert the component Container in Appgyver (as seen in the screen shot) all components on all pages disappear - only the background will be left.

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services as of July 2024
entry.content:
Hi Rudra Mishra,

Data Services is purely an on-premise architecture based product which can also be hosted in the cloud. Datasphere is a Service on the Clod and we should not compare both the products to the extent of a successor/predecessor scope. It is the need of the user/customer whether they want to go for data integration on cloud or on-premise. 

Unfortunately we will not be able to suggest the career path for our users.

Best regards,

Shiba

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow LAC 2024
entry.content:
SAP ALM Roadshow 2024
Edição América do Sul

Procurando aprender mais sobre o portfolio de ALM da SAP?
Desejando entender sobre outras soluções além de SAP Solution Manager 7.2?

O Roadshow de SAP ALM está vindo para a América do Sul! Virá para as localidades de São Paulo, Brasil e  Buenos Aires, Argentina. 

Junte-se conosco nesse evento GRATUITO focado em SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Focused Run e na transição do SAP Solution Manager 7.2 para o SAP Cloud ALM.

Fique atento para as datas e registre-se:
11 de Novembro - São Paulo (Brasil)
19 de Novembro - Buenos Aires (Argentina) 

Abaixo temos alguns dos tópicos que serão abordados:

  • SAP Cloud ALM para RISE with SAP e estratégias de suporte clean core
  • Implementando Empresa Inteligente com o SAP Cloud ALM
  • Automatizando testes com Tricentis
  • Otimização de operações que combinam IA e SAP Cloud ALM
  • Aproveitamento do SAP Focused Run para recursos avançados de monitoramento
  • Transição do SAP Solution Manager para o SAP Cloud ALM

Com certeza teremos também diversas oportunidades e horários para tirar dúvidas e receber feedbacks.
Garanta já seu lugar! Vagas limitadas!
Salve as datas e se registre aqui

Esperamos todos vocês lá!!!!

Mais detalhes em SAP ALM LAC 2024 Roadshow site 

#SAPCloudALM #ALM #ALMSummit #SAPSummit #SAPEvents #SolmanTransition

entry.title: How to fix a checkbox parameter, with two data sets with a beforefactory script. its stuck as false.
entry.content:
This is the first time I have tried making a custom report with two different data sets driven by a parameter.  I feel like I have something missing, when I run this it will only run "WithoutSubQuals" no matter if I check or uncheck the include sub qual checkbox.  Thank you for any ideas you have.

The parameter: Its a checkbox, datatype boolean, default as false, static and not required called "Includesubqual". 

The body Table name is: curriculumItemStatus

the datasets: WithoutSubQuals and WithSubQuals (for both I can run the sql and see results in preview datasets). Both sqls have the same column/field names.

I have a script set as beforefactory:

curriculumItem = reportContext.getReportRunnable().designHandle.getDesignHandle().findElement("curriculumItemStatus");
 
if (BirtComp.equalTo(params["Includesubqual"], true)) 
{ curriculumItem.setProperty( "dataSet", "WithSubQuals" ); }
else 
{ curriculumItem.setProperty( "dataSet", "WithoutSubQuals" ); }

entry.title: Re: How to fix a checkbox parameter, with two data sets with a beforefactory script. its stuck as fa
entry.content: I am using Plateau Report Designer 4.4.1.6

entry.title: Structure Formatting for Receiver in SAP CPI through Groovy
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

So, there was a requirement where the XML Structure was coming with <div></div> in one of the fields and we need to remove that, it cannot be handled by XSLT as @ symbol was expected in email id so with the help of the groovy mentioned in the blog below it was resolved.

Source Structure -

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<Root>
<Root1>
<ADONumber>12345</ADONumber>
<State></State>
<AssignedTo>SRIVASTAVA Nidhi (External) <nidhi.srivastava.ext@XXXXX.eu> </AssignedTo>
<Priority></Priority>
<AdditionalComments>
<div>Comment 5</div>
</AdditionalComments>
</Root1>
</Root>

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/groovy-or-xslt-to-remove-xml-tag-not-values-in-sap-cpi-payload/qaq-p/13873348

1) Now, the issue is when there is a copy paste in the system sending the data instead of <div> <span> is coming.  Something like mentioned below, this is resolved by using XSLT before the groovy mentioned in the blog solution and it is working fine - but email id issue persisting.

<AdditionalComments><span style="color:rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.9);font-size:15px;display:inline !important;">Testing Updates after removing SPAN to DIV</span></AdditionalComments>

2) Also, when there is a mix of manual writing and copy paste in the system the field is coming like 

<AdditionalComments><div>ABC  <span style="color:rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.9);font-size:15px;display:inline !important;">Testing Updates after removing SPAN to DIV XYZ <span style="display:inline !important;">Testing Updates after removing SPAN to DIV</span></span></div></AdditionalComments>

For manual written words <div> tag is coming and for copy pasted words <span> tag is coming.

Not sure if I am able to explain my issue properly, require your expert advice.

Thanks,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Imagine if you could converse with your own business data, leveraging the human-like text responses of a Large Language Model (LLM), and have access to context-aware information to make well-informed decisions. We're excited to introduce a central capability at SAP that grounds LLM responses in business data and provides users with relevant, reliable, and responsible output tailored to their specific needs. By feeding relevant and up-to-date information to an LLM, we reduce model hallucinations - all without the need to finetune the model. 

SAP leverages retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to deliver a central and domain-agnostic service that is easy to use and scales across all business functions. This service also fact-checks LLM-generated statements by tracing information back to its sources, enabling users to act with confidence based on verified data. 

In this blog, let’s dive into two capabilities:  

  • Document grounding, a RAG feature for Joule, SAP’s AI copilot 
  • The Grounding capability in the orchestration module of Generative AI hub to build custom AI applications 

Document grounding: Enhancing the Q&A feature of Joule  

The Document grounding capability allows customers to use their own unstructured or semi-structured documents to leverage the Q&A feature of Joule. By connecting their own repositories, such as SharePoint, customers can gain fast and reliable insights from their business documents, saving time spent on finding, opening, and reading documents. 

Real-world use-case: SAP SuccessFactors is a perfect example of how Document grounding can revolutionize employee self-service capabilities via Joule. Let's say an employee needs to find information on their company's parental leave policy. Instead of sifting through lengthy documents, the employee simply asks Joule: "What is the maximum entitlement for parental leave?" Joule, powered by the Document grounding service, retrieves the relevant information from their company's HR policy documents (previously indexed) and provides them with a concise, accurate answer.

View of the Q&A feature in Joule, enhanced with Document Grounding 

 

Business Impact: This capability offers an intuitive user experience, improves self-service efficiency, and reduces the total cost of ownership (TCO) with 24x7 HR support. It's not just an AI feature; it's a whole new way of empowering teams with knowledge at their fingertips. It can lead to potential efficiency improvements of 70% reduction in employee time needed to investigate HR policies & 81% reduction in time spent by HR on ticket handling, showcasing the tangible impact of this capability. 

But the benefits extend beyond just HR. Imagine a sales executive quickly accessing the latest product specifications, or a finance leader getting real-time insights into company financial policies. By making information more accessible and actionable, Document grounding has the potential to transform decision-making processes across the entire organization.  

What’s under the hood? 

SAP’s Document grounding service leverages RAG to add vital context to LLM responses through a multi-step indexing & retrieval process: 

  1. Indexing Pipeline: This component ingests data from diverse sources, processes it, and splits it into manageable chunks. It then generates embeddings of this data and stores them in the SAP HANA Vector Database. 
  2. Vector Database: The SAP HANA Vector Engine queries these vector embeddings. 
  3. Retrieval: For every user query, vector embeddings are generated using the LLM model and a search in the HANA Vector Database finds relevant text chunks. 

Find more technical and commercial information about Document Grounding in our dedicated Help Portal page. 

A Grounding capability via generative AI hub for your customized AI applications 

While Document grounding is meant to cover Joule scenarios, SAP is also addressing the needs of developer persona who would want to benefit from the RAG capability within their customized AI applications. 

By the end of 2024, the document grounding capability will be fully integrated in the orchestration module of the generative AI hub, alongside capabilities like prompt templating, data masking, and content filter. The mechanism exposed sooner in this article will be applicable via the consumption of the Orchestration service or direct API consumption (indexing and retrieval APIs).  

 Architecture of the Grounding capability inside Generative AI hub

What’s coming next? 

Among many other features, our roadmap through 2025 includesmulti-language support, contextualization, extended list of supported document types and formats as well as documents repositories.  

Also, to enhance the quality of indexing and retrieval, SAP is working on backstage technical layers like Automatic RAG (choosing the best chunking strategy automatically), advanced retrieval techniques, Knowledge Graph integration which comes preloaded with a comprehensive representation of SAP’s business entities (including ABAP tables, CDS views, APIs, and key data models), and will play a critical role in grounding AI models and enabling them to deliver more reliable, context-aware insights.  

Any questions? Feel free to contact Grounding product management.  

Email: sebastien.beghelli@sap.com 

Interested in learning more? Refer to the links below 

  • TechEd 2024 session about Document Grounding and the Grounding capability inside GenAI Hub
  • [SAP BTP Onboarding Series]Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding for Your SAP SFSF. 
  • Document grounding Discovery center page

entry.title: Re: Language based Email Triggering from IAS portal
entry.content:
@Cosmin 

I've just tried a similar code like yours and I was not being able to save either, but I found where the issue is.

In your code, you have [$.defaultLocale], but the correct is ["$.defaultLocale"].

When I made this change, it allowed me to save.

Example:

{
"type": "valueMapping",
"sourcePaths": ["$.defaultLocale"],
"targetPath": "$.locale",
"defaultValue": "EN",
"valueMappings": [
{ "key": ["en_US"], "mappedValue": "EN" },
{ "key": ["vi_VN"], "mappedValue": "VN" },
{ "key": ["ko_KR"], "mappedValue": "KR" },
{ "key": ["ja_JP"], "mappedValue": "JP" },
{ "key": ["zh_CN"], "mappedValue": "CN" },
{ "key": ["fr_FR"], "mappedValue": "FR" },
{ "key": ["pt_PT"], "mappedValue": "PT" },
{ "key": ["nl_NL"], "mappedValue": "NL" }
]
}

Still having some challenges on sending the notification in the correct language, but I hope it helps you somehow.

Regards,

Lucas

entry.title: Re: Building an Analytical List Page with CAP OData V4
entry.content:
Hi @MioYasutake 

I found your article and replicated your steps, but the total did not work too. I could make the totals work for the analytical table, so you can update your post

On the app annotations.cds, this is how I configured it.

 

using CatalogService as service from '../../srv/cat-service';

annotate service.OutboundKPI with @Aggregation.ApplySupported: {
  Transformations       : [
    'aggregate',
    'topcount',
    'bottomcount',
    'identity',
    'concat',
    'groupby',
    'filter',
    'search'
  ],
  GroupableProperties   : [
    ACTION
  ],
  AggregatableProperties: [{Property: COUNTMSGPERACTION}, ],
};

annotate service.OutboundKPI with @(Analytics.AggregatedProperty #countMsgPerAction: {
  Name                : 'countMsgPerAction',
  AggregationMethod   : 'sum',
  AggregatableProperty: COUNTMSGPERACTION,
  @Common.Label       : '{i18n>COUNTMSGPERACTION}'
}, );

annotate service.OutboundKPI with @(UI: {
  SelectionFields: [
    ACTION,
    UZEIT
  ],
  LineItem       : [
    {
      $Type: 'UI.DataField',
      Value: ACTION,
    },
    {
      $Type: 'UI.DataField',
      Value: COUNTMSGPERACTION,
    },
  ],
});

annotate service.OutboundKPI with @UI.Chart: {
  Title              : '{i18n>Messages}',
  ChartType          : #Column,
  DynamicMeasures    : ['@Analytics.AggregatedProperty#countMsgPerAction', ],
  Dimensions         : [
    ACTION
  ],
  MeasureAttributes  : [{
    DynamicMeasure: '@Analytics.AggregatedProperty#countMsgPerAction',
    Role          : #Axis1
  }],
  DimensionAttributes: [
    {
      Dimension: ACTION,
      Role     : #Category
    }
  ],
};

annotate service.OutboundKPI with @UI.PresentationVariant: {
  GroupBy       : [
    ACTION
  ],
  Total         : [
    ACTION,
    COUNTMSGPERACTION
  ],
  Visualizations: [
    '@UI.Chart',
    '@UI.LineItem',
  ]
};

 

 On the app manifest.json, I needed to change the primary and secondary path to take the code from the presentation variant settings

I added the annotation on the db\schema.cds.

 

@cds.persistence.exists
@cds.persistence.calcview
entity CV_MESSAGES_KPI {
    key ACTION      : String(30) @title: '{i18n>Action}';
        @Aggregation.default: #SUM
        COUNTACTION : Integer64  @title: '{i18n>CountAction}';
}

 

And the last step I added this annotation on srv\service.cds

    @readonly
    @Aggregation.CustomAggregate #COUNTMSGPERACTION: 'Edm.Integer'
    entity OutboundKPI                           as projection on CV_OUTBOUND_KPI;

 Take a look at it and let me know if there is any questions

Thank you for your post, it is really clear!

entry.title: crystal viewer parameter loses value on navigation
entry.content:
Using "SAP Crystal Reports runtime engine for .NET Framework (64-bit)" version 13.0.31.4010,

and the corresponding component for VS: "SAP Crystal Reports, version for Microsoft Visual Studio" version 13.0.31.4010.

The CR viewer is CrystalDecisions.Web, Version=13.0.4000.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=692fbea5521e1304

My report has several string type parameters. One of these parameters (called p_stu_cnt) is passed to the report. This param is displayed in the 2nd page header of the report.

When the report is rendered, it shows its value fine (e.g. "100"), but when I navigate (say, go to the next page), the parameter loses its value in the report (shows "0").

Not surprisingly, if I export, say to PDF, the lost value appears int he exported output.

I found an article https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/navigation-in-crystal-viewer-with-parameters/qaq-p/10016698 that suggested setting the ReuseParameterValuesOnRefresh property of the viewer to true (it was false by default). I have done this, but no luck resolving the issue.

The value of this parameter is passed from a string variable, which is prior set to the given dataset's table row count, i.e.

string mQualifyingStudentCount = dtResults.Rows.Count;

then later passed on to the report parameter like so:

string p_stu_cnt = mQualifyingStudentCount.ToString();
report.SetParameterValue("p_stu_cnt", p_stu_cnt);

 

The strange thing is that if I just hardcode of my variable and pass it on to the report param, then the value is not lost! i.e:

string p_stu_cnt = '123";
report.SetParameterValue("p_stu_cnt", p_stu_cnt);

 

Is this a bug??!!

I appreciate your help,

entry.title: Re: Fiori App - Entityset missing
entry.content:
Hi everyone.

I'm facing similar issue on my end and I found it may be related to an issue with a timestamp in table DDANNOLOAD. If you extended the CDS view I_ARLINEITEMS to create new fields in the app Manage Customer Line Items, you need to move this extension in an independent TR, wait for the timestamp in table DDANNOLOAD get updated, than move another TR with your OData service extension.

entry.title: Re: how to convert numbers to dimension member info in sa...
entry.content:
@xyz31 

The easiest way I can think of achieving this will be using the getmember() of the Datasource. If you have a table or a chart, you can just use the datasource of it to get the description of the member and then combine it with Year from Scriptvariable 1. I am assuming you have already done the required manipulation and your ScriptVariable1 is assigned "202311"

You can use something like below

ScriptVariable_1="202411"; // Assuming this is assigned by previous logic

var DS= Table_2.getDataSource();

var Year =ScriptVariable_1.substr(0,4);  
	
ScriptVariable_2=Year+ " " +DS.getMember("Date",ScriptVariable_1).description;

 

Output: I have assigned the ScriptVariable2 to dynamic Text Box which initially shows No value but on click of the button which has the above script

 

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Custom Tasks in Onboarding and Offboarding
entry.content:
@DARREN_S_ABE unfortunately, you can't set a Story Report to trigger when a particular Custom Task concludes -- the Story Report can be scheduled to be run/created at a regular time.

While the Story Report can't be sent or delivered via email, you can include a set of Emails to send a Notification to inform them that a given Story Report has run or completed.

entry.title: The Extensibility Wizard: One Tool to Rule Them All?
entry.content:
When creating clean core extensions for SAP S/4HANA, you are confronted with a vast number of powerful tools. The tools can be divided into four quarters by the dimensions on-stack versus side-by-side and low code versus pro-code, which is shown in the following figure:

The set of available tools may be overwhelming at first sight, so how to find the right one? And – if you found the right tool, how do you find the right API or extension point to extend the application you are interested in?

Of course, there are documents that provide guidance on when to use what (see for example in the document Extend SAP S/4HANA in the cloud and on premise with ABAP based extensions , Chapter 3, “When To Use What”), but it is definitely better to get the guidance right in the system when creation the extension. SAP is investing a lot in implementing guidance in the system. The investment goes along two paths:

  • Investment in AI support (Joule)
  • Investment in wizards

Many new wizards are available in the ABAP platform, for example for creating a new RAP BO or for creating custom fields. For a full picture, I refer to other documents and presentations given during TechEd and Devtoberfest 2024.

In this blog post I want to introduce the Extensibility Wizard. This wizard guides you from the application through the creation of the respective extension. Here is an example (instead of reading the following lines, you can also watch my demo in my TechEd or Devtoberfest session What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility 😞

  1. Assume that you are in your SAP S/4HANA application. For my example I choose the application Manage Journal Entries.
  2. Open the Fiori Launchpad context menu (click your avatar). If the Extensibility Wizard is configures (see link to the documentation with the setup guide at the end of this post), you can see the menu entry Create Extension, and you can click it (Figure 2).

                  
                  Figure 2

  1. The Extensibility Wizard opens and shows the different extensions tasks, that you can create for this application. As of 2208, the following extensions tasks are included:
    1. Custom Fields
    2. Custom Logic
    3. Custom Process
    4. The figure below shows – as a labs preview – also the extensibility task Custom Application, which is not yet available in your 2208 release.

               
               Figure 3

  1. Now let us select the extensibility task Custom Process. The next steps of the wizard now guide us through the creation of a new process in SAP Build Process Integration. As the wizard was started from the Manage Journal Entries application, it knows which triggers are relevant for this application, and offers for example the event JournalEntry Created as the trigger for a new process. As a final step, the wizard creates the new process in SAP Build and let you navigate to SAP Build Lobby and directly into the process. (Please see the complete flow in my session What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility )

I hope that this example gives you an idea on how the Extensibility Wizard works.

As mentioned, in 2208, the Extensibility Wizard includes – apart from the Custom Process – two more tasks:

  • The Custom Field task opens the Custom Fields Fiori app in your SAP S/4HANA tenant, where you can create a custom field. Also here, the business context is prefilled according to the application from where you start the wizard.
  • The Custom Logic task opens the Custom Logic Fiori app in your SAP S/4HANA tenant, where you can create custom logic. Also here, the business context is prefilled.

If you are used to open the Custom Fields and the Custom Logic apps via the UI Adaption Mode (menu entry Adapt UI, as you can see in Figure 2), you can of course still do this.

In future additional extensibility items and features will be added, for example:

  • For the extensibility item Custom Process, additional trigger options will be provided.
  • A new extensibility item Custom Application will be added, which guides you through the creation of a new application with SAP Build Code.

Curious to see the wizard in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud tenant? Configure it today, see the documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud

entry.title: Re: Location/owner of private files in SAP Analytic cloud?
entry.content:
@PM8 

Generally its not possible to see private files of others, but you can find the dependency list for a model including the private files. Goto System > OverView > Dependencies

Select the Model line item and click on Factsheet ( right hand side). This will open another tab with list of all dependent items.

 

On this dependent details page click on dependencies you will see all items and then you can find out the owner details when you navigate the tabs. Then you can ask the user to delete the private files.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

You cannot. Date dimensions can be maintained both manually ( User Managed) and automatically ( System Managed) by the application. But Once set to user Managed you cannot revert back to System Managed.

entry.title: API_BUSINESS_PARTNER
entry.content:
I am using API_BUSINESS_PARTNER to update several supplier withholding taxes in my S/4HANA on-prem system.

For this, we're using OData v2 proxy for internal communication

 

IF go_client_proxy IS NOT BOUND.

TRY. "Creamos el proxy local

go_client_proxy = NEW zcl_v2_proxy_t2( )->create_proxy_local_v2( service_id = lc_service_id

service_version = lc_service_version ).

* lo_resource = go_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set( iv_entity_set_name = lc_entity_set ).

CATCH /iwbep/cx_gateway INTO DATA(lx_gateway).

ev_errors = lx_gateway->get_longtext( ).

ENDTRY.

ENDIF.

Where within each LOOP (suppliers) we're calling the API and setting an update request

lo_resource = go_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set( iv_entity_set_name = lc_entity_set ).

DATA(lo_navigate) = lo_resource->navigate_with_key( is_key_data = ls_key_data ).

lo_update = lo_navigate->create_request_for_update( /iwbep/if_cp_request_update=>gcs_update_semantic-patch ). "PATCH


(ls_business_data and ls_key_data are variables within each loop)

lo_update->set_business_data( is_business_data = ls_business_data

it_provided_property = lw_providedproperty )->execute( )->get_business_data( IMPORTING es_business_data = ls_return_data ).

 

* COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

The first PATCH request goes succesfully, but the second one raises the following error
Parameter "IV_X_SAVE" is "" but it should be "A", we can notice that BUP_GLOBAL_MEMORY_SAVE_X is part of the API call stack but dont know how to override this issue

I would appreciate any help if possible,
Thanks

entry.title: Re: crystal viewer parameter loses value on navigation
entry.content: I was not able to see find known bugs. Can you please refer me to that posting? if this is a bug, it is extremely weird: what does it matter to the viewer how I get the value passed in to the parameter? whether I hardcode it, or whether I obtained it from the associated datatable row count, what does it matter? it is still put into a variable, which then used to pass to the parameter value! so why hardcoding the variable value or not would make a difference??

entry.title: Re: 2024-2H-LMS Release LRN-154487 Browse Library Feature for Enhanced Search - Question about RBP
entry.content: I see the situation is solved, but just to answer your question. The "Access Advanced Library Search" is a permission found in the Learning user role and it's managed in the Learning admin side (System Administration > Security > Role Management).

entry.title: Re: How to fix a checkbox parameter, with two data sets w...
entry.content: @kelli2, you can post this situation here as well: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Custom Report Exchange.

entry.title: Re: about sums when expressions include ISNULL
entry.content:
@ko-hei 

I am not sure how your table is structured but creating 2 restricted measure and 1 calculated measure gave me the below

2 restricted Measures and finally the calculated measure as below

IF(ISNULL([#Actual]) ,[#Plan] ,[#Actual] ), where #Actual and #Plan are restricted measures

Hope this helps..

Nikhil

entry.title: No authorization to access service group, You do not have start authorization for R3TR G4BA
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

I am developing an ABAP Restful Application with a single database table as source. It is a training application, there should be no problem showing you the list of elements I have created using the ABAP RESTful wizard:

When I publish the service binding (OData V4 - UI) and I want to Preview, a browser page appears but I am not able to press the Go neither Create buttons because of the following error:

I deleted the strict ( 2 ); sentence in the behavior definition but that did not help. Note that service definition and service binding has different names because wizard required to use different names. Have you experienced some error like these before?

entry.title: Implementing Tablets that Run SAP EWM.
entry.content:
Hello
I want to implement tablets for my Inventory management team. We used to work with RF but it does not show as many information as we would like, so we have to carry laptops to almost every place we plan to do inventory and analysis. Can we use SAP EWM on a tablet that runs WINDOWS 10-11? We use decentralized ERP-EWM, but we only need EWM in order to visualize more information. 
Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Post processing framework (PPF) on ECC6 EHP8 issues
entry.content: Issue was with my persistent class. I was using a structure and the create_persistent and get_persistent needs to be redefined to store the values in database. Changed that to a database table now and it works as expected.
